 {"id":"bd-k1kx","title":"Implement ntm driver using robot mode API","description":"Task: Implement ntm Driver Using Robot Mode API

Purpose
-------
Implement the unified session driver interface using ntm robot mode,
providing advanced orchestration capabilities beyond the local driver.

ntm Robot Mode API
------------------
ntm exposes a JSON API via command-line flags:

1. --robot-spawn SESSION_NAME
   Create new tmux session with Claude panes
   Returns: {"success": true, "session": "...", "panes": [...]}

2. --robot-status
   Query all session states
   Returns: {"sessions": {...}, "questions": [...], "system": {...}}

3. --robot-health=SESSION
   Get health metrics for session
   Returns: {"agents": {...}, "alerts": [...]}

4. --robot-activity=PANE_ID
   Get activity state for specific pane
   Returns: {"state": "GENERATING", "velocity": 12.5, "confidence": 0.85}

5. --robot-route=SESSION
   Get recommended pane for new work
   Returns: {"recommendation": {"pane_id": "...", "reason": "..."}}

6. --robot-send --pane=PANE_ID --msg=MESSAGE
   Send message to pane
   Returns: {"status": "ok", "delivered": true}

Implementation
--------------

ntm_driver_start_session()
  - Call ntm --robot-spawn with --cc=$parallel for Claude panes
  - Record pane assignments in state
  - Return session info JSON

ntm_driver_get_session_state()
  - Call ntm --robot-activity for pane
  - Map ntm states to unified states
  - Include wait_info if WAITING

ntm_driver_send_to_session()
  - Call ntm --robot-send with pane and message
  - Verify delivery confirmation

ntm_driver_stream_events()
  - Poll --robot-activity periodically
  - Detect state transitions
  - Call callback on significant events

ntm_driver_stop_session()
  - Use tmux kill-session (ntm manages cleanup)

State Mapping
-------------
ntm state      -> Unified state
GENERATING     -> generating
WAITING        -> waiting (with wait_info)
THINKING       -> thinking
STALLED        -> stalled
ERROR          -> error

Health Monitoring Integration
-----------------------------
Start background health monitor:
  while session active:
    health = ntm --robot-health
    for alert in health.alerts:
      handle_alert(alert)
    sleep 10

Advantages Over Local Driver
----------------------------
- Velocity-based detection (more accurate)
- Health monitoring built-in
- Routing for optimal pane selection
- Better stall detection
- Structured error patterns

Testing
-------
- Mock ntm commands for unit tests
- Integration test with real ntm
- Verify state mapping correct
- Verify health alerts handled

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] All driver interface methods implemented
- [ ] State mapping matches unified schema
- [ ] Health monitoring integrated
- [ ] Routing recommendation used
- [ ] Graceful fallback if ntm unavailable","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:45.086444687-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:52:26.596085263-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:52:26.596085263-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented full ntm driver with all interface methods: start_session, stop_session, send_to_session, get_session_state, stream_events, list_sessions, session_alive, interrupt_session. State mapping matches unified schema. Health monitoring integrated with velocity-based detection.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k1kx","depends_on_id":"bd-jm89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:11.685975198-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
